text: Deadlines can be stressful, but effective time management helps you stay calm. Start by breaking large tasks into smaller steps and prioritising them based on urgency and importance. When your workload feels overwhelming, practise scope management: avoid adding unnecessary details or extra requests. If you realise you cannot meet a deadline, communicate early with your manager or team—explain the situation honestly and propose a realistic new date. For longer projects, schedule regular check-ins to update everyone on progress. Remember, clear communication and realistic planning are far more professional than last-minute panic. By applying these strategies, you will handle deadlines confidently without sacrificing quality.
